Converts an image to PackBits format and writes the compressed data to a file.

# Purpose
This Ruby script processes an image file and compresses its pixel data using the PackBits compression algorithm. It uses the `RMagick` library to read the image specified by the first command-line argument. The script iterates over each pixel of the image to determine its intensity and opacity. It then encodes this information into two separate arrays: `data` for intensity and `alpha` for opacity. The script compresses these arrays using PackBits, which is a simple run-length encoding scheme, and stores the compressed data in `packed_data` and `packed_alpha`.

The script outputs the compressed data to a new file with a `.packbits` extension. It writes a header to the file that includes a magic number ('PBPB'), the dimensions of the image, and the size of the compressed data. The compressed pixel data is then written to the file. The script also prints the name of the output file and the size of the compressed image data to the console. This script provides a narrow functionality focused on compressing image data using a specific algorithm and does not define any public APIs or external interfaces.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `RMagick`
